growth_records.medium: Anaerobic D-glucose medium (per Usai et al. 2020); control, no added butanol

up.method: glucose oxidase/peroxidase reaction on cell-free supernatant

growth_records.cite: Costa, Paolo et al. Clostridium cellulovorans Proteomic Responses to Butanol Stress. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.674639. [PMC8336468]
growth_records.snippet: Almost a double amount (0.84 ± 0.18 g/L) of glucose was consumed by C. cellulovorans grown in control condition as compared to 6 g/L butanol-enriched cultures (0.46 ± 0.03 g/L) after 4 h ... formate titer significantly decreased (from 0.30
growth_records.notes: Control (no added butanol) batch fermentation. Uptake = glucose consumed 0.84 ± 0.18 g/L over 4 h (exponential-phase snapshot, glucose oxidase/peroxidase assay); this is a VOLUMETRIC consumption, NOT convertible to mmol/gDW/h because absolute biomass/gDW concentration is only shown graphically (Figure 1A) and not stated numerically in text (a protein:biomass conversion factor of 0.261 g/g is given but absolute protein is not). Secretion yields are per g glucose consumed (Figure 2C): formate 0.33 g/g, acetate 0.13 g/g, butyrate 0.43 g/g; formate titer 0.30 g/L. Ethanol yield reported as 'no significant difference' between conditions but no numeric value given, so omitted. H2 and CO2 are named as fermentation products (Sleat et al. 1984) but not quantified here, so omitted. Specific growth rate (mu) is reported only graphically in Figure 1B without an extractable numeric value; growth_method='reported' but numeric mu left null (not fabricated). No MFA in this paper (proteomics/fermentation-balance study).

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic D-glucose medium (per Usai et al. 2020) supplemented with 6 g/L n-butanol (stress condition)

up.method: glucose oxidase/peroxidase reaction on cell-free supernatant

growth_records.cite: Costa, Paolo et al. Clostridium cellulovorans Proteomic Responses to Butanol Stress. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.674639. [PMC8336468]
growth_records.snippet: 6 g/L butanol-enriched cultures (0.46 ± 0.03 g/L) after 4 h ... Butanol-supplemented cultures showed specific glucose consumption about 2-fold higher than control ... formate titer significantly decreased (from 0.30 g/L to 0.07 g/L) ... 0.2
growth_records.notes: 6 g/L n-butanol stress batch fermentation; butanol acts as stressor and is NOT consumed (<0.35 g/L, 'data not shown'). Total glucose consumed 0.46 ± 0.03 g/L over 4 h (volumetric, glucose oxidase/peroxidase assay). Paper states SPECIFIC glucose consumption is 'about 2-fold higher than control' (lower biomass in this condition), a qualitative ~2x factor only — no absolute qS in mmol/gDW/h stated (biomass/gDW only graphical, Figure 1A), so not computed. Secretion yields per g glucose consumed (Figure 2C): formate 0.15 g/g, acetate 0.29 g/g, butyrate 0.62 g/g; formate titer 0.07 g/L. Compared to control, formate down (0.33->0.15 g/g), acetate up (0.13->0.29 g/g), butyrate up (0.43->0.62 g/g). Ethanol yield unchanged (no numeric value, omitted). H2/CO2 not quantified. mu reported only in Figure 1B (graphical, decreased vs control), no extractable numeric value, so growth_rate left null. No MFA in this paper.
